description: A manner, approach, or guiding course understood through how one follows it is 'keiro'. In travel it answers how or which way, rather than naming the ground underfoot. 'ruela' (path) is that traversable ground, 'shoeka' (route) is the selected course, and 'norae' (method) organizes repeatable action toward a result.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into mid-front 'e'. Hold high-front stressed 'i' as a separate hiatus syllable, then tap the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and round into mid-back 'o'.
usage_notes: The noun can describe a practical approach, a manner of living, or a guiding philosophical way. A physical route still takes 'ruela' or 'shoeka' when its ground or course is the point.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A guiding way is lived in the manner of each present act rather than held only as a destination or claim.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A practiced way of doing something can carry observation and correction across generations without pretending that one method fits every place.

semantic_domains.activity: a manner or approach through which an action proceeds
semantic_domains.wisdom: a guiding course shaped through practice and reflection
